 Ujh, Tawi
Ghaggar
 Shivalik Hills
 320
 Seasonal streams
Based on the table, which statement is most accurate?

so.t: Explanation: According to the table, Ghaggar has the maximum length (320 km) in Punjab among the rivers listed, and it only has seasonal streams as tributaries, making it non-perennial in nature. Option A is incorrect as Sutlej has the maximum length (350 km), not Ravi. Option B is incorrect as Sutlej's major tributary Beas joins it, but this doesn't make it a significant tributary system within Punjab itself. Option D is incorrect as the table shows Beas has tributaries from Shivaliks but not necessarily more than others.

q.t:
With reference to the “United Nations Credentials Committee”, consider the following statements:
1. It is a committee set up by the UN Security Council and works under its supervision.
2. It traditionally meets in March, June and September every year.
3. It assesses the credentials of all UN members before submitting a report to the General Assembly for approval.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
— A Credentials Committee is appointed at the beginning of each regular session of the General Assembly. It consists of nine members, who are appointed by the General Assembly on the proposal of the President. Hence, statements 1 and 2 are not correct.
— The Committee reports to the Assembly on the credentials of representatives.
— The Committee is mandated to examine the credentials of representatives of Member States and to report to the General Assembly thereon. Hence, statement 3 is correct.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. All the currency in circulation is the liability of RBI
2. All the currency in circulation is part of money supply
3. Notes and coins are circulated in the economy only through RBI
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
Currency in circulation consists of Notes and Coins. All the currency notes (except one rupee note) are liability of RBI. But one-rupee notes and coins are printed/minted by Govt. of India and hence are liability of Govt. of India.
All the currency in circulation is not part of money supply. Only that currency in circulation which is with the PUBLIC is part of money supply. The currency with bank is not part of money supply.
Currency in circulation = Currency with public + currency with bank
Even if one-rupee notes and coins are minted/printed by Govt. of India, all the currency notes and coins are circulated in the economy by RBI (as per RBI Act 1934).

q.t:
With reference to the Citizenship, consider the following statements:
1. In India both a citizen by birth as well as va naturalised citizen are eligible for the office of President.
2. The Constitution of India empowers the Parliament to enact a law with respect to the matters related to citizenship.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Explanation
• In India both a citizen by birth as well as a naturalised citizen are eligible for the office of President while in the USA, only a citizen by birth and not a naturalised citizen is eligible for the office of President.
• The Constitution deals with the citizenship from Articles 5 to 11 under Part II. It only identifies the persons who became citizens of India at its commencement (i.e., on January 26, 1950). It does not deal with the problem of acquisition or loss of citizenship subsequent to its commencement. It empowers the Parliament to enact a law to provide for such matters and any other matter relating to citizenship. Accordingly, the Parliament has enacted the Citizenship Act (1955), which has been amended from time to time.

q.t:
Consider the following commissions, which of the following are appointed to deal with the famine situations.
1. Campbell Commission
2. Strachey Commission
3. Lyell Commision
4. McDonnell COmmision
How many of the above given statements is/are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is correct : In 1865-66, a famine engulfed Odisha, Bengal, Bihar, and Madras and took a toll of 20 lakhs of lives with Odisha alone losing 10 lakh lives.
○ It was followed by the appointment of a committee under the chairmanship of Sir George Campbell. It blamed government machinery for the tragedy.
● Statement 2 is correct : In 1880, Lytton appointed a commission under Richard Strachey to formulate general principles and suggest measures of preventive or protective character.
● Statement 3 is correct: Closely following the last famine came the famine of 1896- 97. It affected almost every province though in varying degrees of intensity and the total population affected was estimated at 34 million. A commission presided over by Sir James Lyall, ex-Lt Governor of Punjab, adhered to the views expressed by their predecessors in 1880.
● Statement 4 is correct: Following famine of 1899-1900, Lord Curzon appointed a famine commission led by MacDonnell. In its report in 1901, it summarized principles of relief suggesting variations wherever necessary. The commission emphasized the benefits of a policy of moral strategy, early distribution of advances for the purchase of seed and cattle and sinking of temporary wells. It also advocated the appointment of a famine commissioner in a province when relief operations were expected to be extensive.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. Brahmaputra gorge is an erosional landform
2. Sundarban is an erosional feature
3. Chapora fort cliff is the depositional landform
4. Sandspit is an erosional landform
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is Correct:A gorge is a deep and narrow valley with steep, rocky walls, often formed by the erosion of a river or stream cutting through the landscape.

● Statement 2 is Incorrect: The Sundarbans mangrove forest, one of the largest such forests in the world (140,000 ha), lies on the delta of the Ganges, Brahmaputra and Meghna rivers on the Bay of Bengal. A delta is a landform that forms at the mouth of a river where the river meets a standing body of water, such as a lake or an ocean. Deltas are characterised by sediment deposition and the formation of a network of distributaries.

● Statement 3 is Incorrect: Cliffs are not depositional features. A cliff is a vertical or nearly vertical rock face or escarpment, typically found at the edge of a plateau, ridge, or along a coast. It is an erosional feature due to wave action.

● Statement 4 is Incorrect:A sandspit is a narrow, elongated landform made up of sand and other sediments. It typically extends from the shoreline into a body of water, such as an ocean, bay, or estuary. Sand Spits are dynamic features shaped by the action of waves, currents, and sediment deposition.

q.t: People suffer from byssinosis disease due to exposure to?

so.t:
Explanation-
● Byssinosis: Byssinosis is an occupational disease caused by exposure to cotton dust in inadequately ventilated working environments. It commonly occurs in workers who are employed in fabric and yarn manufacturer industries. ● Coal dust: Long term exposure to coal dust leads to Black lung disease which is a common name for pneumoconiosis.
● Silica dust: Long term exposure to silica dust leads to silicosis. It is a progressive lung disease caused by inhalation of silica.
● Asbestos: Long term exposure to asbestos leads to asbestosis which is a chronic inflammatory lung disease.

q.t:
With reference to the religious history of medieval India, the Sufi mystics were known to pursue which of the following practices?
1. Meditation and control of breath
2. Severe ascetic exercises in a lonely place
3. Recitation of holy songs to arouse a state of ecstasy in their audience
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

so.t:
Exp) Option d is correct. All the statements given above are correct regarding the practices of Sufi mystics. The Sufis were mystics who called for liberalism in Islam. They emphasised on an egalitarian society based on universal love.
Statement 1 is correct. Muraqabah is Sufi meditation. T hrough muraqabah a person watches over their (spiritual) heart and gains insight into the heart’s relation with its creator and its own surroundings.
Statement 2 is correct. Sufi mysticism endeavored to produce a personal experience of the divine through mystic and ascetic discipline.
Statement 3 is correct. Sufism, as the mystical dimension of Islam, preaches peace, tolerance and pluralism, while encouraging music as a way of deepening one’s relationship with the Creator.

q.t: In which one of the following groups are all rabi crops for which the MSP is declared by the Government of India?

so.t:
Explanation: Minimum Support Price (MSP): It is the minimum rate at which farmers sell their crops to the government. This price prevents farmers from market fluctuation and offers stability and income security. It is based on the recommendations of the Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) and declared by the Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA). The correct grouping of Rabi crops with MSP by the Government of India includes Masur, Rapeseed, Safflower, and Barley .
● Rabi Crops: These are sown in winter and harvested in the spring. Examples include Wheat, Masur (Lentil), Rapeseed, Safflower and Barley, etc.
● Kharif Crops: These are sown with the onset of the monsoon and harvested in autumn. Examples include Bajra, Maize, Soybean, Rice, Cotton, Jowar, Groundnut, Urad and Sunflower, etc.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Exposed German submarine warfare plans against neutral American shipping
Revealed German attempts to forge a military alliance with Mexico against the United States
Demonstrated German intelligence penetration of the British communication networks
Showed German plans to use chemical weapons in violation of international law

q.t: The Zimmermann Telegram's revelation was particularly damaging to German diplomatic efforts because it:

so.t:
• Option A (Incorrect): The telegram didn't reveal submarine warfare plans; that was already a known issue causing US-German tensions.
• Option B (Correct): The Zimmermann Telegram, intercepted by British intelligence in January 1917, proposed a German-Mexican alliance. Germany promised to help Mexico recover Texas, Arizona, and New Mexico if Mexico joined Germany in war against the US. This direct threat to US territory was instrumental in turning American public opinion toward war.
• Option C (Incorrect): The telegram actually demonstrated British intelligence capabilities in intercepting German communications, not German penetration of British networks.
• Option D (Incorrect): The telegram didn't address chemical weapons; it was specifically about potential German-Mexican cooperation against the US.

q.t: Which Sikh Guru established the city of Amritsar and also compiled the Adi Granth, laying the foundation for both spiritual and temporal authority in Sikhism?

so.t: Explanation: Guru Arjan Dev Ji (the fifth Sikh Guru) compiled the Adi Granth in 1604 and completed the construction of Harmandir Sahib (Golden Temple) in Amritsar. While Guru Ram Das Ji founded Amritsar, it was Guru Arjan Dev Ji who established it as a major spiritual center. He was the first Sikh Guru to be martyred, which led to the militarization of Sikhism under his successor Guru Hargobind Ji. This makes him crucial in establishing both spiritual (Adi Granth) and temporal (Amritsar as a center) authority.

q.t:
Consider the following countries:
1. Bulgaria
2. Czech Republic
3. Hungary
4. Latvia
5. Lithuania
6. Romania
How many of the above-mentioned countries share a land border with Ukraine?

so.t: — Ukraine shares a border with Belarus, Hungary, Moldova, Poland, Romania, Russia, and Slovakia.

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding "Peer to Peer Lending Platforms" in India:
1. They are regulated by RBI as Non Banking Financial Companies
2. They can lend on their own
3. They provide credit guarantee
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
P2P intermediaries are a class of NBFCs and are regulated by RBI that provides the platform which pairs borrowers and individual lenders. With P2P lending, borrowers take loans from individual investors who are willing to lend their own money for an agreed interest rate. The repayments are also made through the NBFC-P2P which processes and forwards the payments to the lenders who invested in the loan. P2P lending is also called social lending or crowd lending.
 NBFC - P2P shall:
o not raise deposits
o not lend on its own
o not provide any credit guarantee
o not facilitate or permit any secured lending linked to its platform
o shall not provide any assurance for the recovery of loans.
o undertake due diligence on the participants;
o undertake credit assessment and risk profiling of the borrowers and disclose the same to their prospective lenders;
o provide assistance in disbursement and repayments of loan amount;
o render services for recovery of loans originated on the platform.
o all fund transfers shall be through and from bank accounts and cash transaction is strictly prohibited.

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding participation of members in election of “Vice President”:
1. Elected Member of Parliament only.
2. Elected and nominated members of Parliament only.
3. Elected member of State legislative assemblies.
How many of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t: Self-Explanatory

opt.t: It moved the British troops in India from the administration of the Copany to the Crown's direct administration.
It sought to remove certain officers in the British Indian Army from India.
It sought to increase the use of Indian officers at higher posts in the Army.
It enabled the recruitment of officers from other countries in Europe.

q.t: Which of the following best explains the purpose behind the “The Army Amalgamation Scheme” of 1861?

so.t:
Explanation:
● The Army Amalgamation Scheme, 1861 moved the Company’s European troops to the services of the Crown. Further, the European troops in India were constantly revamped by periodical visits to England, sometimes termed as the ‘linked-battalion’ scheme. All Indian artillery units, except a few mountain batteries, were made defunct. All higher posts in the army and the artillery departments were reserved for the Europeans.

q.t:
Consider the following gases:
1. Oxygen
2. Nitrogen
3. Helium
4. Carbon dioxide
How many of the above gases are transparent to incoming solar radiation and opaque to outgoing terrestrial radiation?

so.t:
Gases that absorb infrared radiation are known as greenhouse gases.
Carbon dioxide does not absorb the incoming radiation from the sun which is a form of short wave. However, it does absorb the longwave radiation is reflected terrestrial rays and heat the earth and thus is a GHG and contributes to climate change.
Helium, Nitrogen and oxygen are transparent to both insolation and terrestrial radiation (infrared radiation). which means that they do not absorb or emit solar or infrared radiation.
Hence, option A is the correct answer.
Other Greenhouse gases are
● Water vapour (H2O) is the strongest greenhouse gas. As air becomes warmer, it can hold more moisture or water vapour.
● Methane (CH4) is 30 times stronger than carbon dioxide as an absorber of infrared radiation. Methane, however, is present in smaller concentrations than carbon dioxide, so its net contribution to the greenhouse effect is not as large.
● Nitrous oxide (N2O), a relatively long-lived gas, has increased in atmospheric concentration due mainly to agriculture.
● Ozone (O3) is also a relatively minor greenhouse gas because it is found in relatively low concentrations in the troposphere (the lowest layer of the atmosphere). In the troposphere, it is produced by a combination of pollutants — mostly hydrocarbons and nitrogen oxide compounds.

q.t:
Consider the following case study regarding marine organisms:
Kanhaiya and his friends were playing in the close proximity of a nearby river where they came across a pile of dead marine organisms floating on the surface of the water. With reference to the above case study what could be the possible reasons behind the death of those organisms?
1. Increase in the concentration of Carbon dioxide in water
2. Depletion of oxygen levels in water
3. Increase in the concentration of waste materials including heavy metals in the water.
4. Excess algal growth in water.
How many of the statements given above is/are correct:

so.t:
● Statement 1 is correct: Many marine organisms that produce Calcium Carbonate Shells or Skeletons are negatively impacted by increasing carbon dioxide levels in river water. Increasing concentration is a major reason behind the increase in pH levels in river water which makes it very difficult for marine organisms to survive in acidic water.
● Statement 2 and 4 are correct: Marine organisms do also require oxygen to survive in water, decrease in the concentration of dissolved oxygen hinders metabolism in the organisms ultimately leading to death.
○ Reasons behind low concentration of dissolved oxygen in water are- overuse of fertilisers in agriculture, excessive algae growth from nutrients like Nitrogen and Phosphorus, increase in temperature reduces the amount of Dissolved oxygen content in water, poor weather like cloudy days, windless days also lowers the concentration of dissolved oxygen levels in the water.
● Statement 3 is correct: Industries are always established in the close proximity of rivers so that the waste from the industries can directly be dumped into riverbeds. This waste proves to be carcinogenic to marine organisms as it leads to eutrophication, low dissolved oxygen content, acidification of river water.

q.t:
Consider the following Bhakti Saints:
1. Dadu Dayal
2. Guru Nanak
3. Tyagaraja
Who among the above was/were preaching when the Lodi dynasty fell and Babur took over?

so.t:
Exp)
Option b is correct The Lodhi dynasty fell in 1526 when Ibrahim Lodhi was defeated by Babur at Battle of Panipat.
Dadu Dayal was a religious reformer from Gujarat, born in 1544. Tyagaraja, a renowed composer of Carnatic Music was born in 1767.
Guru Nanak (1469-1539) was preaching when Battle of Panipat took place.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Krishi Sakhi Convergence Program (KSCP):
1. It is one of the components of the 'Lakhpati Didi' program.
2. It aims to impart training and certification of Krishi Sakhi as Para-extension Workers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● The 'Lakhpati Didi' program aims to create 3 crore Lakhpati Didis. One of its dimensions is Krishi Sakhi.
● Krishi Sakhi Convergence Program (KSCP) aims to transform rural India through the empowerment of rural Women as Krishi Sakhi, by imparting training and certification of Krishi Sakhi as Para-extension Workers. This certification course aligns with the objectives of the “Lakhpati Didi” Program. So, statements 1 and 2 are correct.
● The scheme provides training in various agricultural services, including:
○ Organizing farmer field schools: Krishi Sakhis are involved in setting up and managing Farmer Field Schools.
○ Preparation and use of bio inputs: They are trained in preparing and using bio inputs and also in establishing bio input shops.
○ Soil health and moisture conservation practices: Training includes practices for maintaining soil health and conserving moisture.
○ Integrated farming systems: The scheme covers training on integrated farming systems to ensure a holistic approach to agriculture.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Both statements are true, and Statement II correctly explains the long-term consequences of Statement I
Both statements are true, but Statement II does not correctly explain the consequences of Statement I
Statement I is true, but Statement II is false

q.t:
Statement I: The Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact of 1939 contained secret protocols dividing Poland and establishing Soviet and German spheres of influence in Eastern Europe.
Statement II: This pact effectively ended the possibility of a two-front war for Germany throughout World War II.
Which of the following is correct?

so.t:
• Statement I (True): The Nazi-Soviet Non-Aggression Pact, signed on August 23, 1939, did indeed contain secret additional protocols that divided Poland between Germany and the Soviet Union and established spheres of influence in the Baltic states and Eastern Europe.
• Statement II (False): This statement is incorrect because the pact only temporarily avoided a two-front war. Germany invaded the Soviet Union on June 22, 1941 (Operation Barbarossa), creating exactly the two-front war scenario that the pact had initially helped avoid. The pact was a temporary strategic arrangement, not a permanent solution.
• Option A (Incorrect): Statement II doesn't correctly explain long-term consequences since the two-front war did occur.
• Option B (Incorrect): Statement II is factually false, not just inadequately explanatory.
• Option C (Correct): Accurately identifies that Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
• Option D (Incorrect): Statement I is historically documented and true.

opt.t: Both statements are true and Statement II correctly explains Statement I
Both statements are true but Statement II does not explain Statement I
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true

q.t:
Statement I: The Punjabi language received official recognition in Punjab state immediately after India's independence in 1947.
Statement II: Waris Shah's "Heer Ranjha" is considered the greatest love story in Punjabi literature and significantly influenced the cultural identity of Punjab.

so.t:
Explanation: Statement I is false - The Punjabi language did not receive official recognition in Punjab state immediately after India's independence in 1947. Prior to independence, Urdu and Persian were dominant in administration, and Hindu Punjabis often used Hindi, while Punjabi was mainly a spoken language with limited official status. The recognition of Punjabi as the official language of Punjab came after significant political and social movements advocating for a Punjabi-speaking state, culminating in the Punjab Reorganisation Act of 1966. This act reorganized Punjab into linguistic lines and established Punjabi, written in the Gurmukhi script, as the official language of the Indian state of Punjab. Before this, the language question was intertwined with religious and political complexities, including demands by the Sikh community for Punjabi in Gurmukhi to be the medium of instruction and official language, amidst opposition from Hindi-speaking groups.
Statement II is true - Waris Shah's "Heer Ranjha" (18th century) is indeed considered the pinnacle of Punjabi romantic literature and has deeply influenced Punjabi cultural consciousness, folk traditions, and literary development.

q.t:
NBFCs raise their resources from which of the following:
1. Loans from Banks
2. By issuance of bonds in the financial markets
3. Money market
4. Through External Commercial Borrowing (ECB)
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
NBFCs borrow from banks and then lend. They also issue bonds in financial markets to raise money and then this money they lend at a higher interest rate. NBFCs also borrow from abroad through debt financing (called ECB). NBFCs also issue Commercial Papers (CP) in the money market to raise short term money.
Mutual funds also invest in NBFCs which means, NBFCs issue debt papers to mutual funds and then this money they lend.
But, the main wholesale funding sources of the NBFCs comprise mainly of:
 Banks (primarily via term loans and rest through non-convertible debentures and commercial paper); and
 debt mutual funds (via non-convertible debentures and commercial paper)
Debentures are long-term unsecured debt financial instruments (they are similar to bonds in functioning). Some debentures have a feature of convertibility into shares after a certain point of time. The debentures which can't be converted into shares or equities are called non-convertible debentures (or NCDs) and earn a higher interest rate.

opt.t: Direct election for Prime Minister will involve a lot of expenditure on election.
Lok Sabha can remove the Prime Minister even before the expiry of his/her term.
Since the Prime Minister is appointed by the President, there is no need for direct election.
In a Parliamentary democracy only the leader of the majority party in Lok Sabha can become Prime Minister.

q.t: Why is the Prime Minister of India not directly elected by the people? Choose the most appropriate answer?

so.t: • Direct election for the office of Prime Minister is against the ethos of Parliamentary democracy. In a Parliamentary democracy, only the elected representatives from the respective constituencies in Lok Sabha choose their leader to run the government.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs:
Newspaper - Person Associated
1. The Hindu - Shyamji Krishna Verma
2. Swadesamitran - G. Subramaniya Aiyar
3. Indian Mirror. - N N Sen
4. The People - Lala Lajpat Rai
How many of the above given statements is/are correct?

so.t:
Explanation:
● Pair 1 is incorrectly matched: G. Subramania Iyer
● Pair 2 is correctly matched : Swadesamitran was a Tamil language newspaper that was published from the then Madras city from 1882 to 1985. One of the earliest Tamil newspapers and the longest in print, Swadesamitran was founded by Indian nationalist G. Subramania Iyer four years after he had started The Hindu.
● Pair 3 is correctly matched : Indian Mirror by N.N. Sen, Indian Mirror a weekly journal of Calcutta in which social and moral subjects were debated.
● Pair 4 is correctly matched : Lala Lajpat Rai took out a Weekly newspaper “People” from Lahore. He himself was the editor of this paper. It was the mouthpiece of the Servant of People Society that encouraged the youth for service to the nation. The paper rose due to Lalaji’s clear & fearless political comments, stable language and balanced editorship.

opt.t: Both (A) and (R ) are correct and (R ) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R ) are correct but (R ) is not the correct explanation of (A)

q.t:
Two statements labelled as Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are given below. Consider them carefully.
(A) Heat budget refers to net flow of energy into Earth in the form of shortwave radiation and the outgoing infrared long-wave radiation into space.
(R) Clouds, ice and atmospheric particles play a significant role in balancing the heat budget of earth.
Choose the correct option from below:

so.t:
● Statement 1 is correct: Earth's heat-budget or Earth's radiation balance refers to the net flow of energy into Earth in the form of shortwave radiation and the outgoing infrared long-wave radiation into space.
○ Incoming heat is absorbed by the Earth, and outgoing heat escapes the Earth in the form of radiation and both are evenly balanced.
○ Heat budget at the top of the atmosphere must be in a balanced state which in turn, is called Radiative equilibrium.
● Statement 2 is incorrect: 29 % of the solar energy that reaches the top of the atmosphere is reflected back to space by clouds, atmospheric particles, or earth surfaces like sea ice and snow. This heat or energy plays no role in Earth’s system.
○ Besides, about 23 % of incoming solar energy is absorbed in the atmosphere by water vapour, dust, and ozone, and 48% passes through the atmosphere and is absorbed by the surface. Thus, in totality, 71 % of the total incoming solar energy is absorbed by the Earth. Also the surplus energy from tropics is distributed to the higher latitudes.

q.t: Environment Protection Act of 1986 is a significant legislation in India that focuses on the prevention, control and abatement of environmental pollution. This law can be related to which of the following provisions of the Indian Constitution?

so.t:
● Both Fundamental Duties and Directive Principles of State Policy have provisions related to environmental protection:
○ Directive Principles of State Policy: Article 48A- The state shall endeavour to protect and improve the environment and to safeguard the forests and wildlife of the country. This Article was added by 42nd constitutional amendment, 1976.
○ Fundamental Duties: Article 51A(g) states that it is the duty of every citizen of India to protect and improve the natural environment including forests, lakes, rivers and wildlife, and to have compassion for the living creatures.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs:
Traditions
 Communities
1. Chaliha Sahib Festival
 Sindhis
2. Nanda Raj Jaat Yatra
 Gonds
3. Wari-Warkari
 Santhals
Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?

so.t:
Exp) Option a is correct
Pair 1 is correctly matched. Chailo Sahib or Chaliha is a forty-day festival which falls in the months of July - August when Sindhis observe fast for forty days to please their God Jhulelal.
Pair 2 is incorrectly matched. Nanda Devi Raj Jaat Yatra is a festival of Gharwali and Kumaoni people in Uttarakhand. Pair 3 is incorrectly matched. Wari Warkari or Varkari (Pandharpur Wari) is a Maharashtrian festival celebrated by Marathas.

q.t: It was initially established as a large center of learning called 'Mahavihara'. It was the world's first residential university. It attracted scholars from all over India, China, Tibet, Persia, and other parts of Asia. reached the peak of its fame and glory during the reign of the Pala dynasty in the 8th and 9th centuries CE. This is the most likely description of:

so.t:
● Nalanda University was founded in the 5th century CE, in the ancient kingdom of Magadha as a large centre of learning or Mahavihara.
● It was the world's first residential university. It was located near the city of Rajagriha(present-day Rajgir). It became one of the most renowned universities in the world, attracting scholars from all over India, China, Tibet, Persia, and other parts of Asia.
● Though the exact age of the University is still a subject of debate, some historians are of the opinion that it might be even older than what is known today.
● It reached the peak of its fame and glory during the reign of the Pala dynasty in the 8th and 9th centuries CE. It was during this period that the university saw global exposure.
● It was designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2016.

q.t:
Consider the following statements about the Continental Congress during the American Revolution:
Statement I: The Second Continental Congress had the constitutional authority to declare independence and conduct foreign relations.
Statement II: The Articles of Confederation created a stronger federal government than what existed under the Continental Congress.
Statement III: The Continental Congress successfully negotiated the Treaty of Paris (1783) without formal constitutional authority to conduct foreign policy.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
• Statement I (False): The Second Continental Congress had no formal constitutional authority. It was an extra-legal body that assumed powers out of necessity during the revolutionary crisis. It acted without any constitutional basis, deriving its legitimacy from the revolutionary situation rather than legal authority.
• Statement II (False): The Articles of Confederation actually created a weaker federal structure than what had evolved under the Continental Congress during wartime. The Articles deliberately limited federal power, creating a "firm league of friendship" rather than a strong central government.
• Statement III (True): This is the paradox of the Continental Congress. Despite having no formal constitutional authority, it successfully conducted foreign relations, declared independence, managed the war effort, and negotiated the Treaty of Paris in 1783. The Congress acted as a de facto government through revolutionary necessity.
• Option A (Incorrect): Statement I is false.
• Option B (Incorrect): Statement II is false.
• Option C (Correct): Only Statement III is accurate.
• Option D (Incorrect): Statements I and II are both false.

opt.t: He was the first Sufi saint to write poetry in Punjabi and his verses are included in the Guru Granth Sahib.
He founded the city of Faridkot and served as its first ruler during the Mughal period.
He was a political leader who led resistance against Islamic invasions in Punjab.
He introduced Persian as the official language of Punjab under Delhi Sultanate rule.

q.t: Baba Farid, a renowned 13th-century Sufi saint, is significant in Indian history because:

so.t:
Answer: A) He was the first Sufi saint to write poetry in Punjabi and his verses are included in the Guru Granth Sahib.
Explanation:
Baba Farid (Fariduddin Ganjshakar) played a pioneering role in using the Punjabi language for spiritual poetry, which greatly contributed to the development of Punjabi literature. He is also one of the Sikh Bhagats whose hymns were incorporated into the Guru Granth Sahib, underscoring his influence on Sikhism. Faridkot city is named after him, reflecting his lasting spiritual legacy in Punjab. His poetry and teachings bridged Islamic Sufi mysticism with local devotional traditions, impacting the cultural synthesis in medieval India

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding "Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs):
1. They are regulated by respective Real Estate Regulatory Authorities (RERA) of every State
2. It will make real estate sector accessible to small investors
Select the correct answer using the code given below

so.t:
A “Real estate investment trust" is a trust registered under the Indian Trusts Act, 1882 which manages a fund/ corpus where the funds are invested in real estate property. REITS are mutual fund like institutions that enable investment into the real estate sector by pooling small sums of money from multitude of individual investors. REITS are regulated by Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI).
Most middle-class investors presently do not invest in commercial real estate because of the big size of investment. This entry barrier will be removed through REITs as it will make the expensive real estate sector accessible to the middle-class investor. REITS will also help the real estate industry which is currently plagued with problems such as weak demand, cash constraints, stuck projects etc. Now, the developers will be able to sell their property to REITs and move on to execution of new projects.
SEBI has also approved Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs) along with REITs which are very similar to REITs but are for infrastructure sector.

q.t:
Consider the following Statements with respect to “Privilege Motion”:
1. Rules of Both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha Regulates Privilege Motion.
2. The Speaker/Chairman can decide on the privilege motion himself or herself or refer it to the privileges committee of Parliament.
3. Privilege Committee can give the punishment of Imprisonment for the breach of Privileges.
How many of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Explanation
• Rule No 222 in Chapter 20 of the Lok Sabha Rule Book and correspondingly Rule 187 in Chapter 16 of the Rajya Sabha rulebook governs privilege. It says that a member may, with the consent of the Speaker or the Chairperson, raise a question involving a breach of privilege either of a member or of the House or of a committee thereof. The rules however mandate that any notice should be relating to an incident of recent occurrence and should need the intervention of the House. Notices have to be given before 10 am to the Speaker or the Chairperson. Thus statement 1 is correct.
• The Speaker/RS chairperson is the first level of scrutiny of a privilege motion. The Speaker/Chair can decide on the privilege motion himself or herself or refer it to the privileges committee of Parliament. If the Speaker/Chair gives consent under Rule 222, the member concerned is given an opportunity to make a short statement. Thus statement 2 is correct.
• Statement 3 is Correct. They can give Punishment.

opt.t: Satyendranath Tagore became the first Indian to succeed in the Indian Civil Services Examination.

q.t: Which among the following events happened earliest?

so.t:
Explanation:
● Statement 1. Swami Dayanand established Arya Samaj in 1875
● Statement 2 : Neeldarpan was written by Dinabandhu Mitra - 1858-59 (During the Indigo Revolt)
● Statement 3. Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay wrote Anandmath in 1882
● Statement 4 Satyendranath Tagore became the first Indian to succeed in the Indian Civil Services Examination- 1863 (Surendranath Banerjee, Subash Chandra Bose also cleared ICS in later years)

opt.t: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I
Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-1
Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding Inter Tropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ)
Statement I - ITCZ is a low pressure zone characterised by high insolation which has influence on Indian monsoon
Statement II - Southward movement of ITCZ cause onset of monsoon while it’s Northward movement cause dry condition
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

so.t:
Explanation:
Statement I is correct - The air at the Inter Tropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ) rises because of convection caused by high insolation and a low pressure is created. The winds from the tropics converge at this low pressure zone.
Influence of ITCZ on Indian monsoon
Statement II is incorrect- During summer, the ITCZ shifts northward, drawing moist air from the Indian Ocean, leading to the onset of the monsoon and heavy rainfall. In winter, the ITCZ moves south, causing dry conditions. The interplay between the ITCZ and the monsoon is crucial for India's climate variability.

Additional Movement:
● During the month of March and September, sun is overhead the equatorial area in tropics. This leads to intense heating which creates a belt of low pressure region. This low pressure belt attracts the north-east trade winds from northern hemisphere and south-east trade winds form southern hemisphere. Convergence of these two trade winds in this belt leads to ascending to air which creates a low pressure situation. This low pressure belt this is known as Inter-tropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ).
● With the change in the apparent position of sun towards tropic of cancer, ITCZ changes its position. In July, the ITCZ is located around 20°N-25°N latitudes (over the Gangetic plain), sometimes called the monsoon trough. This monsoon trough creates low pressure area over north and northwest India. Due to this shift of ITCZ, the south-east trade winds of the southern hemisphere cross the equator between 40° and 60°E longitudes. These trade winds change their direction due to the Coriolis force and start blowing from southwest to northeast. Therefore, it is known as ‘southwest monsoon’.
● In winter, due to apparent movement of sun toward tropic of Capricorn, the ITCZ moves southward, and so the reversal of wind direction takes place in Indian sun-continent. Now, the wind blows from northeast to southwest. Therefore, winter monsoon is known ‘northeast monsoon’.

q.t:
Consider the following statement about Genetically Engineered tree:
1. In India the first experiment with genetically engineered trees was with Coconut.
2. At present, the commercial cultivation of GM trees is not allowed in India
3. In India, the use of GM trees is regulated under the Environment Protection Act, 1986
How many of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
Explanation:
● Statement 1 is incorrect: In India the first experiment with genetically engineered trees was with Rubber trees developed by the Rubber research institute in Kerala.
● Statement 2 is correct: At present, the commercial cultivation of GM trees is not allowed in India
● Statement 3 is correct: In India, the use of GM trees is regulated under the Environment Protection Act, 1986, and the Rules for the Manufacture, Use, Import, Export, and Storage of Hazardous Microorganisms/Genetically Engineered Organisms or Cells, 1989
Additional information:
● Genetic Engineering Appraisal Committee (GEAC) is responsible for granting approval for the field trials of GM trees in India.
● The GEAC considers various factors, such as the potential risks to the environment and human health, before granting approval for field trials.

q.t: Banjaras during the medieval period of Indian history were generally are

so.t:
Exp) Option d is correct
The Banjaras are historically a nomadic community, migrating from one place to another in search of trade. They are now scattered across various states of India. Their origins are believed be rooted in Rajasthan, northern India. The Banjara people are known by different names — in Andhra Pradesh, Lambada or Lambadi; in Karnataka, Lambani; in Rajasthan, Gwar or Gwaraiya

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. India currently imports about half of its annual requirement of bitumen.
2. India’s Bitumen consumption has decreased in the last five years due to the use of organic materials as alternatives.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Bitumen is a black substance produced through the distillation of crude oil and is widely used to bind the surfaces of paved roads.
● India currently imports about half of its annual requirement of bitumen. India imported 3.21 million tonnes of bitumen in 2023-24 worth ₹11,033 crore. India's indigenous bitumen production in the last fiscal year was 5.24 million tonnes. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Bitumen consumption has gone up significantly in recent years in line with increasing road construction in India - the average annual consumption in the last five financial years was 7.7 million tonnes, up from 5.94 million tonnes between 2014-15 and 2018-19. So, statement 2 is not correct. Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

opt.t: The disproportionate tax burden relative to their voting power in the Estates-General
Their numerical majority in terms of population percentage
The unequal representation in the number of delegates allocated
The complete tax exemption enjoyed by the clergy

q.t:
The following table shows the composition of the Estates-General called by Louis XVI:
Estate
 Population %
 Tax Burden %
 Voting Power
 Representatives
First Estate
 1%
 0%
 33.3%
 ~300
Second Estate
 2%
 Minimal
 33.3%
 ~300
Third Estate
 97%
 ~80%
 33.3%
 ~600
Based on this information, which factor most directly contributed to the Third Estate's decision to form the National Assembly?

so.t:
Explanation:
• Option A (Correct): The fundamental grievance was that despite bearing approximately 80% of the tax burden and representing 97% of the population, the Third Estate had only 33.3% voting power due to the traditional system where each Estate voted as a unit. This meant the First and Second Estates could always outvote the Third Estate 2-1, despite the Third Estate's overwhelming demographic and economic importance.
• Option B (Incorrect): While the Third Estate represented 97% of the population, this alone wasn't the decisive factor. The issue was how this majority was translated (or rather, not translated) into political power.
• Option C (Incorrect): Actually, the Third Estate had double the number of representatives (600 vs. 300 each for the other estates), but this was meaningless under the traditional voting system where each estate voted as a unit.
• Option D (Incorrect): While clerical tax exemption was a grievance, it was the systemic voting inequality that directly prompted the formation of the National Assembly. The Third Estate demanded voting by head rather than by estate.

opt.t: Akali movement based on linguistic reorganization
Punjabi Suba movement based on religious reorganization
Language movement based on the three-language formula
Regional movement based on economic considerations

q.t: The formation of Punjab state in its current boundaries in 1966 was primarily the result of which movement and principle?

so.t: Explanation: The current Punjab state was formed in 1966 through the Punjabi Suba movement led by the Akali Dal, but it was officially based on linguistic reorganization principle (Punjabi-speaking areas), not religious grounds. Though the movement had religious undertones, the government accepted it on linguistic grounds to avoid setting a precedent for religion-based state formation. This led to the creation of Haryana (Hindi-speaking) and Punjab (Punjabi-speaking) from the erstwhile larger Punjab state.

opt.t: The output of goods and services
The amount of money needed to purchase a given quantity of goods and services

q.t: If a country is experiencing inflation, then what will decrease:

so.t: When a country faces inflation, we require more money to purchase a given quantity of goods and services because the purchasing power of rupee decreases. In case of inflation generally wages increase but nothing can be said about the output. And in case of inflation, the amount of money needed will be more to purchase the same goods and services. So, (d) option is true.

q.t:
With respect to the “Presidential Address” of the House, consider the following statements:
1. The Constitution makes it incumbent upon the President to address both Houses of Parliament assembled together at the commencement of the first Session after each General Election to the Lok Sabha and at the commencement of the first Session each year.
2. No Member can raise questions on the Address by the President.
3. Discussion on matters referred to in the Address takes place on a Motion of Thanks moved by a Member and seconded by another Member.
How many of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
• The Constitution provides for an Address by the President to either House or both Houses assembled together [Article 86(1)]. The Constitution also makes incumbent upon the President to address both Houses of Parliament assembled together at the commencement of the first Session after each General Election to the Lok Sabha and at the commencement of the first Session each year and inform Parliament of the causes of its summons. [Article 87(1)].
• The matters referred to in the Address by the President to the Houses are discussed on a Motion of Thanks moved by a Member and seconded by another Member.
• No Member can raise questions on the Address by the President. Any action on the part of a Member which mars the occasion or creates disturbance is punishable by the House to which that Member belongs. Discussion on matters referred to in the Address takes place on a Motion of Thanks moved by a Member and seconded by another Member.
• The scope of discussion on the Address is very wide and the functioning of the entire administration is open for discussion; the limitations inter alia are that Members should not refer to matters which are not the direct responsibility of the Government of India, and the name of the President should not be brought in during the debate since the Government, and not the President, is responsible for the contents of the Address. Thus, all the Statements are Correct.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs
Humidity
 Characteristics
1. Dew point
 Actual amount of the water vapour present in the atmosphere
2. Absolute humidity
 Percentage of moisture present in the atmosphere as compared to its full capacity
3. Relative humidity
 Temperature at which air becomes completely saturated
How many pairs are correctly matched ?

so.t:
Explanation:
● Statement 1 is incorrect - Dew Point is the temperature where water vapour starts to condense out of the air (the temperature at which air becomes completely saturated). Above this temperature the moisture stays in the air.
● Water vapour present in the air is known as humidity. It is expressed quantitatively in different ways.
● Statement 2 is incorrect - The actual amount of the water vapour present in the atmosphere is known as the absolute humidity. It is the weight of water vapour per unit volume of air and is expressed in terms of grams per cubic metre.
● Statement 3 is incorrect - The percentage of moisture present in the atmosphere as compared to its full capacity at a given temperature is known as the relative humidity. With the change of air temperature, the capacity to retain moisture increases or decreases and the relative humidity is also affected. It is greater over the oceans and least over the continents.

q.t: It is a type of manipulation activity that involves artificially inflating the price of a stock through false and misleading information, only to sell the stock at the inflated price and leave investors with significant losses. It is particularly prevalent in the micro-cap and small-cap sectors, where companies often have limited public information and trading volumes are lower. This is the most likely description of:

so.t:
Explanation:
● In the stock market, a pump and dump scheme is a type of manipulation activity that involves artificially inflating the price of a stock through false and misleading information, only to sell the stock at the inflated price and leave investors with significant losses. This manipulative tactic is particularly prevalent in the micro-cap and small-cap sectors, where companies often have limited public information and trading volumes are lower.
● The individuals who buy the stock at inflated prices typically face substantial losses when the stock price crashes. These schemes undermine confidence in the financial markets, making legitimate investors wary of potential fraud.
○ Participants in pump and dump manipulation can face severe legal penalties, including fines, disgorgement of profits, and imprisonment.
○ Under the Securities and Exchange Board of India’s (SEBI) guidelines, pump and dump schemes are completely banned.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Superior European intellectual capabilities and scientific rationalism
The unique combination of coal deposits, colonial markets, and institutional frameworks
The Protestant work ethic and capitalist mentality absent in other cultures
Advanced European agricultural techniques that freed labor for industrial work

q.t: The "Great Divergence" between Western Europe and other civilizations during the Industrial Revolution is most accurately attributed to:

so.t:
Explanation:
• Option A (Incorrect): This reflects outdated Eurocentric thinking. Other civilizations, particularly China and the Islamic world, had demonstrated significant intellectual and scientific achievements. The divergence wasn't due to inherent European intellectual superiority.
• Option B (Correct): Modern historians identify a combination of factors: accessible coal deposits (especially in Britain), colonial markets providing both raw materials and consumers, and specific institutional frameworks (property rights, financial systems, patent laws) that encouraged innovation and investment. This combination was historically specific rather than culturally determined.
• Option C (Incorrect): Max Weber's Protestant ethic thesis has been largely discredited by comparative historical analysis. Many non-Protestant societies showed similar entrepreneurial drive and capitalist development when conditions were favorable.
• Option D (Incorrect): While agricultural improvements did free labor for industry, this occurred in many societies without leading to industrial revolution. Agricultural productivity alone was insufficient without the other institutional and resource factors.

opt.t: All mentioned personalities were primarily associated with violent revolutionary activities
The time periods show a continuous nationalist movement in Punjab spanning nearly 80 years
Religious reform movements preceded political movements in Punjab's nationalist struggle
Master Tara Singh was the only leader who continued activism post-independence

q.t:
Study the following data about Punjab's freedom fighters and their contributions:
Freedom Fighter

 Revolutionary
 Assassinated Michael O'Dwyer

Master Tara Singh
 Akali movement

Based on the information, which statement is most accurate?

so.t: Explanation: The table shows nationalist activities from 1880s (Lala Lajpat Rai) to 1960s (Master Tara Singh), spanning nearly 80 years of continuous struggle. Option A is incorrect as not all were violent revolutionaries (Lala Lajpat Rai used non-violent methods). Option C is incorrect as the table doesn't show religious reform preceding political movements. Option D is incorrect as the table doesn't confirm others didn't continue post-independence activities, and Master Tara Singh's major post-independence work was the Punjabi Suba movement.

opt.t: Both statements are true and Statement II correctly explains the consequences of Statement I
Both statements are true but Statement II does not explain Statement I
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true

q.t: Statement I: The Green Revolution in Punjab began in the 1960s and primarily focused on increasing wheat and rice production through high-yielding varieties. Statement II: Punjab's current agricultural crisis includes issues of soil degradation, water table depletion, and farmer indebtedness partly due to the Green Revolution's long-term effects.

so.t: Explanation: Statement I is true - the Green Revolution in Punjab started in the 1960s with the introduction of high-yielding varieties of wheat and rice, along with chemical fertilizers and pesticides. Statement II is true and correctly explains the long-term consequences - intensive monoculture, excessive use of chemicals, and over-extraction of groundwater have led to soil degradation, declining water tables, and increased input costs causing farmer indebtedness. This demonstrates how initial agricultural success created subsequent sustainability challenges.

q.t:
Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in India can happen by which of the following ways:
1. Subsidiary
2. Joint Venture
3. Acquisition of shares
Select the correct answer using the
code given below:

so.t:
Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) happens in three ways: -
 A foreign company purchasing shares of Indian company
 A foreign company in collaboration with an Indian company establishing a new company called Joint Venture Company in India
 A foreign company establishing a subsidiary or a child company in India

FDI investors are not allowed to purchase debt securities.

q.t:
Which of the following statements are incorrect, with reference to Panchayati raj elections in India?
1. The elections are conducted by an independent election commission appointed by the president of India.
2. One third of the seats are reserved for scheduled castes and scheduled tribes.
3. The age limit to contest in the elections is 25 years.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

so.t: Elections for local bodies are conducted by State Election Commission. The SEC is appointed by governor of the state. In these elections, the seats reserved for SC and STs are in proportion to their population in the constituency. The minimum age to contest in these elections is 21 years. Hence, all the statements above are incorrect.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Dead Sea in the Middle East has high salinity due to intense evaporation in its arid climate.
2. Rapid change in salinity in vertical oc water column up is called halocline
3. The Atlantic ocean has less salinity than Mediterranean sea.
4. Bay The Arabian sea has less salinity than the of Bengal.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is correct - Dead sea has high salinity due to intense evaporation in its arid climate. Dead Sea is the saltiest water body on earth and is also the deepest hypersaline lake in the world. It is 9.6 times more saline than ocean water. ○ Other reasons for high salinity in the dead sea are: Chemical nature of rocks which dissolves during rain and adds up to the salinity of the sea. Also, Fresh water which used to go into the dead sea previously is now diverted to other regions.
● Statement 2 is correct - Halocline is a vertical zone in the oceanic water column in which salinity changes rapidly with depth, located below the well-mixed, uniformly saline surface water layer.
● Statement 3 is correct - As the Mediterranean Sea is a semi enclosed sea it has restricted flow of water and more evaporation which led to more salinity as compared to the Atlantic Ocean.
● Statement 4 is correct- Arabian sea with less number of rivers falling into it as compared to Bay of Benagl has more salinity.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA):
1. It is a statutory body constituted under the Companies Act, 2013.
2. It monitors and enforces compliance with accounting and auditing standards for companies.
3. The Union Minister of Corporate Affairs is the ex-officio chairperson of NFRA.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● The National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) is a statutory body constituted in 2018 by the Government of India under the Companies Act, 2013. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Functions and Duties: As per the Companies Act, 2013, the duties of the NFRA are to:
○ Recommend accounting and auditing policies and standards to be adopted by companies for approval by the Central Government;
○ Monitor and enforce compliance with accounting standards and auditing standards. So, statement 2 is correct.
○ Oversee the quality of service of the professions associated with ensuring compliance with such standards and suggest measures for improvement in the quality of service;
○ Perform such other functions and duties as may be necessary or incidental to the aforesaid functions and
duties.
● Composition of the NFRA:
○ The Companies Act, 2013 requires the NFRA to have a chairperson who will be appointed by the Central Government and a maximum of 15 members. So, statement 3 is not correct.
○ The appointment of such chairperson and members are subject to the following qualifications:
■ They should be having an expertise in accountancy, auditing, finance or law.
■ They are required to make a declaration to the Central Government that there is no conflict of interest or lack of independence in their appointment.
■ All the members including the chairperson who are in full-time employment should not be associated with any audit firm (including related consultancy firms) during their term of office and 2 years after their term.

opt.t: Both statements are true, and Statement II correctly explains the nature of Statement I
Both statements are true, but Statement II contradicts Statement I
Statement I is false, but Statement II is true

q.t:
Statement I: The New Economic Policy (NEP) introduced by Lenin in 1921 represented a complete abandonment of socialist principles in favor of free-market capitalism.
Statement II: The NEP allowed private trade and small-scale private ownership while maintaining state control over heavy industry and foreign trade.
Which of the following is correct?

so.t:
• Statement I (False): The NEP was not a complete abandonment of socialist principles but rather a strategic retreat or "breathing space" as Lenin called it. The state maintained control over the "commanding heights" of the economy (heavy industry, banking, foreign trade, transportation). It was a mixed economy, not a return to pure capitalism.
• Statement II (True): This accurately describes the NEP. It permitted private trade, small-scale manufacturing, and private farming while the state retained control over major industries, banks, and foreign commerce. This was Lenin's compromise between ideological purity and economic necessity after the disasters of War Communism.
• Option A (Incorrect): Statement I is false, so this option is invalid.
• Option B (Incorrect): Statement I is false; the statements aren't both true.
• Option C (Correct): Accurately identifies that Statement I mischaracterizes the NEP while Statement II correctly describes it.
• Option D (Incorrect): Statement II is factually accurate about the NEP's structure.

opt.t: Only Statements I and II are correct
Only Statements II and III are correct
Only Statements I and III are correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements about Punjab's industrial sector:
Statement I: Punjab's industrial policy focuses primarily on agro-based industries to leverage its agricultural strength.
Statement II: The state has significant textile and sports goods manufacturing, particularly in Jalandhar and Ludhiana.
Statement III: Punjab's service sector contributes more to the state's GDP than the industrial sector.

so.t: Explanation: All statements are correct. Statement I is true - Punjab's industrial policy emphasizes agro-processing, food processing, and agriculture-related industries. Statement II is true - Ludhiana is known as the "Manchester of India" for textiles and Jalandhar is famous for sports goods manufacturing, leather products, and hand tools. Statement III is true - like most Indian states, Punjab's service sector (including trade, transport, banking, and public administration) contributes more to GDP than manufacturing, reflecting the structural shift in the economy.

q.t:
Consider the following statement with respect to “Urban Governance”:
1. The 74th Constitutional amendment does apply to Union Territory only when the President directs so.
2. District planning committee shall consult such institutions as the Governor specify for drafting developmental plans.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
• Statement 1:- It applies with modification if the President directs, otherwise it applies automatically.
Every District Planning Committee shall, in preparing the draft development plan, have regard to:-
1. Matters of common interest between the Panchayats and the Municipalities including spatial planning, sharing of water and other physical and natural resources, the integrated development of infrastructure and environmental conservation.
2. The extent and type of available resources whether financial or otherwise.
3. Consult such institutions and organisations as the Governor may, by order, specify.
• The Chairperson of every District Planning Committee shall forward the development plan, as recommended by such Committee, to the Government of the State.

q.t:
Arrange the following statements in decreasing order of their coastline:
1. Maharashtra
2. Karnataka
3. Odisha
4. West Bengal
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t: Option D is correct: The coastline of India is 5422.6 km. Gujarat has the longest coastline in comparison to the other coastal states followed by Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and Maharashtra.

q.t:
Arrange the following states in ascending order of the foreign direct investment received for the year 2023-24:
1. Maharashtra
2. Gujarat
3. Karnataka
Select the correct answer using the code given -below:

so.t:
● As per the data released by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Maharashtra received Foreign direct investment (FDI) amounting to Rs 1,25,101 crore in 2023-24. While it is higher than any other state, it is also higher than Rs 1,18,422 crore that the state received in 2022-23.
● Maharashtra has attracted the highest foreign direct investment for the second consecutive year in 2023 24. The state emerged on top in attracting FDI in 2022-23 too.
● The state received more FDI than the combined sum of second-ranked Gujarat and third-ranked Karnataka. While Gujarat witnessed a 62 per cent increase in FDI over the previous year, FDI in Karnataka has declined. Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Punjab State Cooperative Supply and Marketing Federation (MARKFED)
Punjab State Cooperative Agricultural Development Bank (PCADB)

q.t: Which cooperative institution in Punjab is specifically associated with the procurement, processing, and marketing of agricultural produce, and has played a crucial role in supporting farmers during the 2025 flood crisis?

so.t: Explanation: MARKFED (Punjab State Cooperative Supply and Marketing Federation) is the apex cooperative institution responsible for procurement, processing, and marketing of agricultural produce in Punjab. During the 2025 floods, MARKFED played a crucial role in crop loss assessment, providing immediate relief to farmers, and facilitating insurance claims. While other options are important cooperative institutions, MARKFED specifically deals with agricultural marketing and has been instrumental in flood relief operations.

q.t:
Arrange the following tributaries of Indus in the direction of North to South?
1. Chenab
2. Jhelum
3. Beas
4. Ravi
5. Satluj
Select the correct answer using the code given below::

so.t:
The Indus River System is one of the largest river basins of the world. Its main tributaries are Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, Beas and Satluj. The source of origin of the indus is near Mansarover Lake in Tibet. It enters India through Ladakh and further enters Pakistan near Chillas. The ‘Panjnad’ is the joint river current of all the five rivers of Punjab i.e., the Satluj, the Beas, the Ravi, the Chenab and the Jhelum.
Jhelum- it originates from the Sheshnag lake near Verinag. It reaches Pakistan through a narrow gorge after flowing through the Wular lake which is near Srinagar . It joins Chenab in Pakistan.
Ravi originates near the Rohtang pass in Kullu hills of Himachal Pradesh. It is the tributary of the Chenab.
Chenab- It is made of two streams the Chandra and the Bhaga which meet at Tandi in Himachal Pradesh. The chenab is the largest tributary of Indus.
Beas- it originates near the Rohtang pass and it joins Satluj near the place called Harike in Punjab. Indira Gandhi Canal has been drawn out from the confluence of the two rivers at Harike barrage. Satluj- originates from the Rakas lake near the Mansarover lake.

q.t: Casgevy and Lyfgenia are primarily used to cure which one of the following diseases?

so.t:
● Sickle cell disease: It is a group of inherited blood disorders. The primary problem in sickle cell disease is a mutation in Haemoglobin, a protein found in red blood cells that delivers oxygen to the body’s tissues.
○ This mutation causes red blood cells to develop a crescent or “sickle” shape. These sickled red blood cells restrict the flow in blood vessels and limit oxygen delivery to the body’s tissues, leading to severe pain organ damage called vaso-occlusive events (VOEs) or vaso-occlusive crises (VOCs).
○ The recurrence of these events or crises can lead to life-threatening disabilities and/or early death.
● Treatments:
○ Casgevy, a cell-based gene therapy, is approved for the treatment of sickle cell disease in patients 12 of age and older with recurrent vaso-occlusive crises. It is the first United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved therapy utilising CRISPR/Cas9, a type of genome editing technology. Patients’ hematopoietic (blood) stem cells are modified by genome editing using CRISPR/Cas9 technology.
○ Lyfgenia is a cell-based gene therapy. It uses a lentiviral vector (gene delivery vehicle) for genetic modification and is approved for the treatment of patients 12 years of age and older with sickle cell disease and a history of vaso-occlusive events.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Both statements are true and Statement II provides a solution to the problem in Statement I
Both statements are true but Statement II does not address Statement I
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true

q.t:
Statement I: Punjab's public finance has been strained due to high subsidies on electricity, water, and fertilizers provided to the agricultural sector.
Statement II: The 15th Finance Commission recommended reducing revenue deficit and improving tax-to-GDP ratio for Punjab's fiscal consolidation.

so.t:
Statement I is true because Punjab's public finance has been significantly strained due to high subsidies on electricity, water, and fertilizers provided to the agricultural sector. The government provides free or highly subsidized electricity and irrigation water to farmers, and fertilizer subsidies also add to the fiscal burden, causing stress on the state's finances.
Statement II is also true. The 15th Finance Commission (2021-26) recommended measures for fiscal consolidation for states, including Punjab. It advised reducing the revenue deficit and improving the tax-to-GDP ratio to address fiscal imbalances. These recommendations aim to improve Punjab's fiscal health and provide a framework to manage subsidies and overall public expenditure more sustainably.
Thus, Statement II provides a solution or pathway to address the fiscal strain highlighted in Statement I

q.t: Which of the following projects is built on Chenab river?

so.t:
Option a is incorrect: The Pong Project has been built on the confluence of river Beas. The Pong reservoir is in Himachal Pradesh. The name of the reservoir is Maharana Pratap Sagar.
Option b is incorrect: The Uri project has been executed on the river Jhelum in the district of Baramulla in Jammu and Kashmir.
Option c is incorrect: The Chamera power project is in Himachal Pradesh on the River Ravi. Option d is correct: The Salal Project has been executed on Salal river in Himachal Pradesh.

q.t: The term ‘Casimir Effect’ is related to:

so.t:
● Casimir effect: It is the effect arising from the quantum theory of electromagnetic radiation in which the energy present in empty space might produce a tiny force between two objects.
○ The effect was first postulated in 1948 by Dutch physicist Hendrik Casimir.
● It is a quantum phenomenon in which two materials, when placed close together, can be attracted or repelled by quantum fluctuations.
● Working of Casimir Effect:
○ The working principle of the Casimir effect is based on the quantum fluctuations of the electromagnetic field in the vacuum. These fluctuations result in the temporary appearance of virtual particles, which continuously emerge and disappear.
○ When two uncharged conducting plates are placed very close together, typically within a micron apart, these fluctuations become constrained between the plates.
○ This leads to a decrease in the energy density of the vacuum between the plates compared to the surrounding vacuum.
○ As a consequence, the plates experience a net attractive force, pulling them closer together.
○ This leads to an imbalance in the radiation pressure exerted by the vacuum fluctuations on the plates, with a stronger pressure exerted on the outside of the plates compared to the inside.
● Example of Casimir Effect:
○ When colloidal particles are suspended in a vacuum, they experience the Casimir force due to their proximity to other surfaces.
○ This force, which can be either attractive or repulsive depending on the separation distance and the dielectric properties of the surrounding medium, influences the motion and interactions of the colloidal particles.
○ For example, if two particles are close enough, they may experience an attractive Casimir force that pulls
them together, leading to aggregation.
○ In materials science, for instance, the formation of nanoparticle clusters can significantly alter the properties of composite materials.
○ Similarly, in biotechnology, the aggregation of nanoparticles can influence the design and efficacy of drug delivery systems or biomolecular interactions.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Only Statements I and II are correct
Only Statements I and III are correct
Only Statements II and III are correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements about Punjab's economic growth and development:
Statement I: Punjab's per capita income is among the highest in India, primarily due to agricultural prosperity and remittances from diaspora.
Statement II: The state's industrial growth has been constrained by land acquisition issues and competition from neighboring states.
Statement III: Punjab's human development indicators, particularly in education and health, are significantly better than the national average.

so.t:
Explanation:
• Statement I: Punjab's per capita income is indeed among the highest in India, substantially supported by agricultural prosperity due to its role as the "breadbasket of India." Additionally, remittances from the Punjabi diaspora contribute significantly to the state's economy, especially rural areas. This makes Statement I true.
• Statement II: Punjab's industrial growth has been constrained due to multiple factors, notably land acquisition issues stemming from farmers' resistance and legal challenges, along with stiff competition from more industrially advanced neighbouring states like Haryana and Gujarat. Hence, Statement II is true.
• Statement III: Although Punjab’s human development indicators such as education and health have improved over time, they are not significantly better when compared with the national average. In fact, some indicators show Punjab's performance lagging or only marginally better, with increasing regional disparities within Punjab as well. Hence, Statement III is false or at least overstated.

opt.t: Only one pair is correctly matched
Only two pairs are correctly matched
Only three pairs are correctly matched
All the pairs are correctly matched

q.t:
Consider the following pairs:

How many of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
Explanation:
Pair 1 is correctly matched- The Tanakpur project has been executed at Tanakpur (Uttarakhand) on the river Kali that flows on the Indo-Nepal border.
Pair 2 is incorrectly matched- The Gandak project is a joint project of Bihar, Uttar Pradesh and Nepal. Hydro electricity is produced at Suratpura (Nepal) on the river Gandak.
Pair 3 is correctly matched- The Rihand project is mainly of Uttar Pradesh but Bihar and Madhya Pradesh also benefit from it. Bihar gets water which reaches the Son canal through Son river. This Project has been executed at Pipri, on the river Rihand, a tributary of Son. Pair 4 is correctly matched- The Matatila project is a joint project of Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh on the river Betwa. The dam has been built in Uttar Pradesh.

q.t: It is a part of the Cyclades archipelago in the Aegean Sea and designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. It is said to be "doomed to disappear" within decades due to rising sea levels and geological processes. This is the most likely description of:

so.t:
Delos island:
● The UNESCO heritage site of Delos is one of the most important sanctuaries of the ancient Greek and Roman world.
○ Surrounded by piercing azure waters, Delos' 2,000-year-old buildings offer a microcosm of information on daily life during the Hellenistic and Roman periods. It is a rocky island, part of the Cyclades archipelago in the Aegean Sea, was first settled in the 3rd millennium B.C.
● The site is said to disappear within decades, because of rising sea levels brought about by climate change. Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Both statements are true and Statement II was influenced by the principles established in Statement I
Both statements are true but Statement II is unrelated to Statement I
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true

q.t:
Statement I: The Khalsa Panth was established by Guru Gobind Singh in 1699 at Anandpur Sahib, institutionalizing the martial character of Sikhism.
Statement II: The Gurdwara Reform Movement (1920-1925) was primarily aimed at removing British administrative control from Sikh religious institutions.

so.t: Explanation: Statement I is true - Guru Gobind Singh established the Khalsa Panth in 1699, institutionalizing the Five Ks and martial character. Statement II is true - the Gurdwara Reform Movement (Akali Movement) aimed to remove corrupt mahants and British-supported administration from Sikh shrines. Statement II was influenced by Statement I because the Khalsa principles of sovereignty and self-governance established by Guru Gobind Singh inspired Sikhs to seek control over their religious institutions, making the connection meaningful.

q.t:
Consider the following paragraph:
This river flows across the Deccan Plateau from the Western to the Eastern Ghats. It forms an inter State boundary between the States of Telangana and Maharashtra; and Telangana and Chattisgarh. This river flows through the States of Maharashtra, Telangana, Chhattisgarh, and Andhra Pradesh. At \ Dowlaiswaram, the river divides into two branches, the Gautami and Vasishta. It is the longest river of Peninsular India.
The river referred in above paragraph is:

so.t:
Option C is the correct answer- Godavari is the longest river of peninsular India. It is known as Dakshin Ganga or Vridha Ganga ( old Ganga) because of its length , age and size. It originates from a place called Trimbakeshwar in the Nashik district of Maharashtra. It is navigable in delta regions. Its main tributaries are Indravati, Pranhita, Purna, Dudhna, Manjra etc.
○ At Dowlaiswaram, the river divides into two branches, the Gautami and Vasishta. Between the two, lies the Godavari Central Delta.
○ The Gautami branch joins the Bay of Bengal flowing through the Yanam enclave of Union Territory of Puducherry.

q.t: GREAT scheme is sometimes seen in the news in the context of:

so.t:
● National Technical Textiles Mission (NTTM)’s Grant for Research and Entrepreneurship across Aspiring Innovators in Technical Textiles (GREAT) encourages young innovators, scientists/technologists, and startup ventures in the field of technical textiles to translate their ideas into commercial technologies/products and make India self-reliant.
● It supports individual entrepreneurs or start-ups for functional prototypes or commercialization of their technologies for technical textiles.
● Government of India is providing a maximum fund support of INR 50 lakhs per startup. Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Arrange the following rivers in North to South direction-
1. Mandovi
2. Periyar
3. Sharavati
4. Gangavali
5. Pampa
Select the correct answer using the code given below::

so.t:
Explanation:
● Mandovi river originates in Western Ghats, passes through Karnataka, Goa and Maharashtra and drains into Arabian Sea.
● Gangavali river originates from south of Dharwad and passes through Karnataka.
● Sharavati river originates near Shimoga, Karnataka and passes through Karnataka.
● Periyar river has its source in Sivagiri hills, Kerala. It discharges in Vembanad lake.
● Pampa river is the 3rd longest river of Kerala; it also discharges in Vembanad lake.
Additional Information:
● West flowing rivers from North to South-
○ Ghaggar-Hakra river – Luni — Sabarmati — Mahi — Narmada — Tapti — Mandovi — kali — Gangavali — Sharavati — Beypore — Bharathapuzha — Periyar — Pampa .

q.t:
Consider the following:
1. Absence of essential nutrients
2. Intensive agricultural practices along the coastal regions
3. Increase in level of atmospheric nitrogen
4. Rise in algal decay
How many of the above factors are responsible for the formation of dead zones in oceans?

so.t:
Dead zones are low-oxygen, or hypoxic, areas in the world’s oceans and lakes. Because most organisms need oxygen to live, only few organisms can survive in hypoxic conditions. That is why these areas are called dead zones.
